entries.summary: # AI Command Prompts These are natural-language commands for Claude Code, OpenClaw, Codex, or other agents. ## Export a project into the vault

entries.title: Architecture
entries.summary: # Architecture AI Memory Vault is intentionally simple: Git is the sync layer; Markdown/YAML are the agent-readable data layer; a small CLI provides deterministic helpers. ## Components

entries.title: Borrowed Ideas Roadmap
entries.summary: # Borrowed Ideas Roadmap This document tracks useful patterns found in adjacent open-source AI memory, Claude Code, MCP, and skill projects. It is not a dependency list; it is a product roadmap for ideas worth adapting into AI Memory Vault.

entries.title: GitNexus Ideas for AI Memory Vault
entries.summary: # GitNexus Ideas for AI Memory Vault This document captures ideas worth borrowing from GitNexus-style code intelligence for AI Memory Vault. > Note: GitNexus itself appears to use a non-commercial license in some integrations' license notes

entries.title: Usage

entries.title: Coding Style and Verification
entries.summary: # Coding Style and Verification - Keep functions small and focused. - Avoid hardcoded credentials and environment-specific secrets.

entries.title: Global AI Instructions
entries.summary: # Global AI Instructions These instructions are intended to be claimed by AI coding agents. - Act autonomously for reversible local development tasks.

entries.title: Global Mistakes and Lessons
entries.summary: # Global Mistakes and Lessons Store recurring mistakes only after they have been verified and generalized. Format:

entries.title: User Preferences Template
entries.summary: # User Preferences Template This file is a template. Agents should update it only with user-approved durable preferences. - Communication language: update when known.

entries.title: Global Workflows
entries.summary: # Global Workflows Store repeatable workflows that apply across many projects. ## Vault project export

entries.title: Promoted Proposals
entries.summary: # Promoted Proposals Reviewed proposals are moved here after `ai-vault promote` appends their durable content into canonical project memory. Keep this folder as an audit trail. Do not place raw transcripts or secrets here.

entries.title: Proposal Inbox
entries.summary: # Proposal Inbox Non-destructive summarize proposals land here first. Agents should review, redact, and promote durable knowledge into `projects/`, `patterns/`, or `skills/` only after validation.

entries.title: Inbox
entries.summary: # Inbox Staging area for non-destructive AI memory work before promotion into canonical vault files. - `proposals/` — summarize proposals generated by `ai-vault summarize`.

entries.title: Session Summaries Inbox
entries.summary: # Session Summaries Inbox Append-only staging area for short session summaries. Keep summaries concise. Do not store raw transcripts or secrets. Promote only durable, reusable knowledge into canonical vault assets.

entries.title: VAULT_PROTOCOL.md — AI Memory Vault Protocol
entries.summary: # VAULT_PROTOCOL.md — AI Memory Vault Protocol This repository is designed to be maintained primarily by AI agents. Any agent working with this vault MUST follow this protocol before reading, writing, claiming, or exporting memory.
